comments: Approved. The contract scope is explicitly bounded to generating exactly three markdown design/manifest deliverables: tasks/v0.6-m6-a-state-separation-design.md, tasks/v0.6-m6-a-comparative-synthesis.md, and tasks/v0.6-m6-a-cleanup-manifest.md. The allowed_files list matches that boundary, and the stop conditions forbid modifying files outside that set as well as any attempt to delete or move existing files. The out_of_scope clause correctly prohibits execution of deletions, large-scale migrations, and file stubbing, and it further blocks modification to workflow, source, or execution files. The recon packet is consistent with this contract boundary: it frames M6-A as design/analysis only, describes migration and stubbing only as deferred or future work, and states that no deletions or migrations occur until M6-B. I see no authorization here for broad repo migration, file movement, or destructive cleanup during M6-A.
